% Known-truth DGP cells: canonical_low_action, canonical_high_action

\section{Generated Known-Truth Results}
This section is generated by \texttt{tdccp\_run.py} from the \texttt{experiments.known\_truth} harness. TD-CCP is transition-free for structural parameter estimation: the estimator uses observed state-action-next-state tuples to estimate the recursive terms \(h(a,x)\) and \(g(a,x)\). Known transitions enter only after estimation, when the recovered reward is evaluated for policies, values, and counterfactuals.

The low-dimensional action-dependent cell is the current validated path and passes all hard gates. The high-dimensional encoded-state cell is included as a diagnostic stress test. It uses the encoded semi-gradient basis, but it does not yet pass the same structural recovery gates, so TD-CCP should not be described as fully migrated on high-dimensional known-truth DGPs.

\subsection{Hard Flexible DGP Showcase}
The hard flexible DGP uses \texttt{ShapeshifterEnvironment} for stochastic transitions and frozen neural state features, but keeps the TD-CCP paper's finite structural utility form. The reward is linear in an orthonormalized neural basis interacted with nonbaseline actions; action 0 is the utility normalization. This makes \(\theta\), reward, policy, value, \(Q\), and counterfactual recovery meaningful gates.

The paper-faithful hard flexible DGP passes all gates. This is the condition under which the RTD page may describe the finite-theta hard-case showcase as validated.

\end{table}

\subsection{Raw Neural Reward Diagnostic}
The raw neural/neural shapeshifter case is retained as a diagnostic failure artifact. It uses a frozen neural reward matrix with no finite true \(\theta\). TD-CCP is therefore not claimed to recover the raw reward matrix in this case; policy and counterfactual gates are reported separately from the finite-theta showcase.

Failed diagnostic gates: converged, reward\_normalized\_rmse, value\_normalized\_rmse, q\_normalized\_rmse.
